title: Runtime Request Schemas, Negotiation, Problem Details, and OpenAPI · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent university archive receives a different publisher mix, unknown XML shape, validator state, tenant, async schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers runtime request schemas, negotiation, problem details, and openapi into a changed runtime-validated documented API boundary. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of runtime-validated documented API boundary with automated and judgment evidence. Learners pr…
objectives: Validate params, query, headers, body, content type, size, cardinality, and unknown fields before constructing a command.
Define string, number, boolean, date, enum, array, empty, repeated, and default coercion explicitly and test ambiguity.
Map stable domain and infrastructure causes to bounded RFC 9457 types, statuses, field evidence, correlation, and retry guidance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Request Unknown Admission
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 5346 during an out-of-order Promise and AbortSignal rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove runtime request admission, coercion, problem details, negotiation, and OpenAPI conformance; every external value must remain unknown until current runtime validation admits it; then reorder one timer, Promise, claim, lease, fencing, worker, disconnect, or shutdown settlement and exclude stale effects. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Request Unknown Admission.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for validate params, query, headers, body, content type, size, cardinality, and unknown fields before constructing a command. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Request Unknown Admission, keep Feed Ts Request Unknown Admission connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
